channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Shanghai's Offshore Finance Initiative Gains Support from Global Financial Institutions
item.description: The Action Plan for Developing Offshore Finance in Shanghai International Financial Center, released during the 2026 Lujiazui Forum, outlines strategies to position Shanghai as a leading offshore fina...

item.title: Tundu Lissu's Legal Proceedings Highlight Tanzania's Democratic Challenges
item.description: Tundu Lissu, a prominent Tanzanian opposition leader and member of Chadema party, appeared at the High Court amid heightened security and public interest. His court appearance marks another chapter in...

item.title: AJK Election Commission Announces Three-Phase Voting Schedule Starting July 27
item.description: The Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) Election Commission has announced that elections in the region, originally scheduled for July 27, will now be conducted in three phases ending on August 10. The decisi...

item.title: U.S. military strikes Iran amid escalating Mideast conflict and Gulf state retaliation
item.description: The U.S. has intensified its military operations in the Mideast, launching strikes against Iran for the 10th consecutive night. This follows a reported attack on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz, a cr...

item.title: Russian Frigate Conducts Live-Fire Exercise Off UK Coast Amid Naval Monitoring
item.description: A Russian frigate, the Neustrashimy, conducted a live-fire artillery exercise approximately 40 nautical miles south of Plymouth, England, on Monday. The operation, lasting around 30 minutes, was monit...

item.title: Influencers Misinterpret Studies, Spread False Claims About Sunscreen and Cancer Risk
item.description: Dermatologists recommend sunscreen as a top way to prevent skin cancer, but summer 2026 heatwaves have led some social media users to falsely claim commercial sunblocks can trigger the disease. Expert...

item.title: KPMG appoints longtime executive to lead recovery from audit scandal
item.description: KPMG has appointed John Sams, a long-serving employee with over two decades at the firm, to spearhead its recovery from a major audit leaks scandal. Sams, who joined KPMG as a graduate in 2003, holds ...

item.title: Analysis of Trump's 2 a.m. Rage Tweeting and Its Implications
item.description: The article discusses how Trump's late-night tweeting, driven by anxiety and political pressure, has become a significant aspect of his public persona. It highlights the author's concerns about the po...

item.title: Progress of Vice President Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte continued on its eighth day, with ongoing proceedings in the Senate. The live updates provided by ABS-CBN News on July 21, 2026, highlighted key mo...

item.title: Spain Wins 2026 FIFA World Cup with Historic Victory
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, held across North America, concluded with Spain clinching the title after a dramatic final against Argentina. Spain's defensive record of conceding just one goal set a new ben...

item.title: Kenyatta University Graduate Advocates for Chinese Language Studies Amid Rising Opportunities
item.description: A Kenyatta University graduate who studied Chinese language opened up about why he chose the course at his graduation ceremony. The graduate argued that the increasing number of Chinese nationals comi...

item.title: New PM Andy Burnham outlines measures to tackle cost-of-living crisis and political instability
item.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham has outlined his initial plans to address the UK's cost-of-living crisis, improve living standards, and end a decade of political instability. Following his appointment as ...

item.title: Madlanga commission continues investigating criminal justice system corruption
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry, established to probe allegations of criminality, political interference, and corruption within South Africa's criminal justice system, is continuing its hearings. O...

item.title: Kelly Clarkson Shares Humorous Story About Health Scare from Contaminated Food During Vegas Show
item.description: Kelly Clarkson humorously recounted an awkward health scare during her Las Vegas residency, joking about suspecting cyclosporiasis after a recent show. The singer, 44, described rushing back to her ho...

item.description: The Chief Election Commissioner of Azad Jammu and Kashmir, Ghulam Mustafa Mughal, has announced that elections in the region will be conducted in three phases starting on July 27, 2026. The first phas...

item.title: Khampepe commission continues hearings into TRC cases
item.description: The Khampepe commission of inquiry, tasked with investigating alleged attempts to delay the prosecution of apartheid-era perpetrators, is holding hearings in Newtown, Johannesburg. The proceedings, wh...

item.title: Syd's New Album 'Beard' Marks a Mature Phase in Her Career
item.description: Syd, the Los Angeles-based artist, discusses her third solo album 'Beard', reflecting on her evolution from a cult figure to a Grammy-winning songwriter. The album, described as a 'coming-of-age roman...

item.title: Madonna's Rep Denies Rumors of Presence at Trump's World Cup Suite
item.description: Madonna's representatives have refuted claims that the singer was present in Donald Trump's luxury suite during the World Cup final. The rumor originated from a brief shot of Trump's box during the Sp...

item.title: Kileleshwa MCA Warns ODM May Be Aiding Gachagua Against Ruto's Government
item.description: Kileleshwa Member of County Assembly (MCA) Robert Alai has raised concerns about the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) potentially undermining President William Ruto's administration, allegedly in favo...

item.title: Philippines defends rights in West Philippine Sea amid China tensions
